core.label: Python Fundamentals
core.description: Core syntax, data types, and basic program structure.

topics.label: Installation & REPL
topics.description: Install Python and use the interactive interpreter to run code. Understanding the REPL accelerates experimentation.

topics.label: Variables & Data Types
topics.description: Learn int, float, str, bool, and None. Python is dynamically typed, so understanding type behavior is essential.

topics.label: Strings & Formatting
topics.description: Master string methods, slicing, and f-strings. String manipulation is one of the most common tasks in Python.

topics.label: Operators & Expressions
topics.description: Arithmetic, comparison, logical, and bitwise operators. These form the building blocks of all program logic.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/elif/else, for loops, while loops, break, continue, and pass. Control flow governs how your program makes decisions.

topics.label: Lists & Tuples
topics.description: Ordered sequences — mutable lists and immutable tuples. These are the most used data structures in Python.

topics.label: Dictionaries & Sets
topics.description: Key-value mappings and unique collections. Dicts are used everywhere in Python for fast lookups.

topics.label: List Comprehensions
topics.description: Concise syntax for creating lists from iterables. Comprehensions are idiomatic Python and improve readability.

topics.label: Input & Output
topics.description: Read user input and print formatted output. Fundamental for interactive scripts and debugging.

topics.label: Basic Debugging
topics.description: Use print statements and the built-in debugger (pdb) to trace issues. Essential skill from day one.

core.label: Functions & Modular Code
core.description: Writing reusable code with functions, modules, and packages.

topics.label: Defining Functions
topics.description: Create functions with def, parameters, and return values. Functions are the primary unit of code reuse.

topics.label: Arguments & Parameters
topics.description: Positional, keyword, default, *args, and **kwargs. Flexible argument handling makes functions more powerful.

topics.label: Scope & Closures
topics.description: Understand local, enclosing, global, and built-in scope (LEGB rule). Scope bugs are common for beginners.

topics.label: Lambda Functions
topics.description: Anonymous single-expression functions. Useful with map, filter, sorted, and as callbacks.

topics.label: Modules & Imports
topics.description: Organize code into modules and packages. The import system is central to Python project structure.

topics.label: Standard Library Overview
topics.description: Explore os, sys, json, datetime, collections, itertools, and more. The stdlib saves you from reinventing the wheel.

topics.label: Docstrings & Help
topics.description: Write documentation strings and use help(). Good docstrings make code self-documenting.

topics.label: Recursion
topics.description: Functions that call themselves for divide-and-conquer problems. Important for algorithms and tree traversals.

topics.label: Higher-Order Functions
topics.description: Functions that take or return other functions — map, filter, reduce, and functools. Foundation for functional patterns.

core.label: Object-Oriented Programming
core.description: Classes, objects, inheritance, and Python's data model.

topics.label: Classes & Objects
topics.description: Define classes with __init__, attributes, and methods. OOP is the backbone of most Python libraries.

topics.label: Instance vs Class vs Static Methods
topics.description: Understand self, @classmethod, and @staticmethod. Each serves a different purpose in class design.

topics.label: Inheritance & MRO
topics.description: Single and multiple inheritance, super(), and method resolution order. Enables code reuse across class hierarchies.

topics.label: Properties & Descriptors
topics.description: Use @property for computed attributes and control access. Properties keep your API clean while adding logic.

topics.label: Dunder Methods
topics.description: Implement __str__, __repr__, __eq__, __len__, __getitem__, etc. Dunder methods integrate your classes with Python's syntax.

topics.label: Abstract Base Classes
topics.description: Define interfaces with abc.ABC and @abstractmethod. ABCs enforce contracts in class hierarchies.

topics.label: Dataclasses
topics.description: Use @dataclass for boilerplate-free data containers. Dataclasses reduce repetitive __init__ and __repr__ code.

topics.label: Enums
topics.description: Define named constants with enum.Enum. Enums prevent magic values and improve code clarity.

topics.label: Slots & Memory
topics.description: Use __slots__ to reduce memory usage in classes with many instances. Important for performance-sensitive code.

core.label: Error Handling & File I/O
core.description: Exception handling, file operations, and working with data formats.

topics.label: Exceptions & Try/Except
topics.description: Handle runtime errors gracefully with try/except/else/finally. Proper error handling prevents crashes in production.

topics.label: Custom Exceptions
topics.description: Create domain-specific exception classes. Custom exceptions make error handling more expressive.

topics.label: Context Managers
topics.description: Use with statements and write custom context managers. They guarantee cleanup of resources like files and connections.

topics.label: Reading & Writing Files
topics.description: Open, read, write, and append to text and binary files. File I/O is essential for data processing scripts.

topics.label: Working with JSON
topics.description: Serialize and deserialize data with the json module. JSON is the standard for API communication and config files.

topics.label: Working with CSV
topics.description: Read and write CSV files with the csv module. CSV is ubiquitous in data exchange and reporting.

topics.label: Pathlib
topics.description: Use pathlib.Path for cross-platform file path manipulation. Pathlib is cleaner than os.path for modern Python.

topics.label: Logging
topics.description: Replace print debugging with the logging module. Logging provides levels, formatting, and output control for production.

topics.label: Regular Expressions
topics.description: Pattern matching with the re module. Regex is powerful for text parsing, validation, and search-replace.

core.label: Iterators, Generators & Decorators
core.description: Advanced Python patterns for elegant and efficient code.

topics.label: Iterators & Iterables
topics.description: Understand the iterator protocol (__iter__, __next__). Iteration is at the heart of Python's for loops and comprehensions.

topics.label: Generators
topics.description: Create lazy sequences with yield. Generators are memory-efficient for processing large datasets.

topics.label: Generator Expressions
topics.description: Inline generator syntax similar to list comprehensions. Use them for memory-efficient pipelines.

topics.label: Decorators
topics.description: Wrap functions with @decorator syntax to add behavior. Decorators are used everywhere — Flask routes, caching, auth, etc.

topics.label: functools Utilities
topics.description: Use lru_cache, partial, wraps, and reduce from functools. These tools complement decorators and functional patterns.

topics.label: itertools
topics.description: Chain, product, permutations, combinations, and more. itertools provides efficient building blocks for iteration.

topics.label: collections Module
topics.description: Counter, defaultdict, OrderedDict, namedtuple, deque. Specialized containers for common patterns.

topics.label: Type Hints
topics.description: Annotate functions and variables with types using typing module. Type hints improve IDE support and catch bugs early.

core.label: Testing & Code Quality
core.description: Writing tests, linting, and maintaining code standards.

topics.label: unittest
topics.description: Python's built-in test framework with TestCase classes. Understanding unittest is important since many projects use it.

topics.label: pytest
topics.description: The most popular Python testing framework with fixtures and plugins. pytest's simplicity makes testing enjoyable.

topics.label: Mocking
topics.description: Use unittest.mock to isolate units under test. Mocking external dependencies makes tests fast and reliable.

topics.label: Code Coverage
topics.description: Measure test coverage with coverage.py. Coverage reports reveal untested code paths.

topics.label: Linting with Ruff
topics.description: Use Ruff (or flake8/pylint) to enforce code style and catch errors. Linters catch bugs before they reach production.

topics.label: Code Formatting
topics.description: Auto-format code with Black or Ruff formatter. Consistent formatting eliminates style debates in teams.

topics.label: Static Type Checking
topics.description: Run mypy to verify type annotations at build time. Catches type errors without running the code.

topics.label: Pre-commit Hooks
topics.description: Automate linting and formatting on every commit. Pre-commit prevents bad code from entering the repository.

core.label: Packaging, Environments & Concurrency
core.description: Virtual environments, dependency management, and concurrent programming.

topics.label: Virtual Environments
topics.description: Isolate project dependencies with venv. Virtual environments prevent dependency conflicts between projects.

topics.label: pip & Requirements
topics.description: Install packages and pin versions with requirements.txt. Proper dependency management ensures reproducible builds.

topics.label: Poetry / uv
topics.description: Modern dependency management with lock files. Poetry and uv provide deterministic installs and better workflows.

topics.label: Building Packages
topics.description: Create distributable packages with pyproject.toml and setuptools. Packaging lets you share and reuse your code.

topics.label: Threading
topics.description: Run I/O-bound tasks concurrently with the threading module. Useful for network requests and file operations.

topics.label: Multiprocessing
topics.description: Bypass the GIL with process-based parallelism. Multiprocessing is the way to use multiple CPU cores in Python.

topics.label: Asyncio
topics.description: Write asynchronous code with async/await. Asyncio excels at handling thousands of concurrent I/O operations.

topics.label: concurrent.futures
topics.description: High-level interface for threading and multiprocessing with ThreadPoolExecutor and ProcessPoolExecutor.

topics.label: The GIL
topics.description: Understand the Global Interpreter Lock and its impact on threading. Knowing the GIL explains why multiprocessing exists.
